review: I walked by a city electrical or some sort of cable box, and it was wide-open with wires were hanging everywhere. But when you’re in the app, there’s no category for this. review: The app is a cool idea and works great for submitting issues, but there is no detail or communication after you submit. My case was just marked “closed” one day and there were no details. Even a single sentence would be nice. That was disappointing as I had hope for this app.

review: This is a waste of taxpayer dollars. The city does no follow up or take action on abandoned car reports. After 60 days it all expires and you get a message saying “no action possible” and case closed. Why would the city would spend money on an app to report things when it doesn’t have capacity to solve anything? Not surprising, but still frustrating.

review:

review: Location map doesn’t allow search. Very few categories compared to the variety of issues facing our city. Only 3 stars on app store. Sorry to be so critical, but there is no excuse for such a poorly managed app.

review: Other than reporting damaged or not working streetlights, nothing else works.

review: First off, location works great for me. I reported flooding on a sidewalk and the city got back to me almost immediately. Wow! However, someone decided that a flooded sidewalk is only caused by a blocked drain, so they routed my request to Seattle Public Utilities. They came out, noted that the drain was not blocked and is, in fact, higher than the water because the street has sunk - so they cleaned the drain anyway and closed the ticket. The next time it rained I reported it again. This time someone at SPU wrote a full page letter on letterhead, scanned it, and sent me a PDF to tell me that I needed to call the streets people. Your tax dollars at work. They could make the call themselves, or spend a lot more time telling me to do it. I never asked for a drain to be cleaned in the first place. So an A grade for responsiveness and an F- for problem solving.
title: Good app - now fix the city

review: Can’t sign out
title: Can’t sign out

review: You’re kidding right?
title: App support?

review: The reviews ‘Is Anyone There?’ And ‘ ...Useless’ are very unfair about the great effort Seattle is making to keep our City pulled together. I get that they didn’t get an immediate response but I can’t see how they don’t understand that it’s a big city and things get addressed only as the resources are available - that’s true for everybody on a personal level and more so on an institutional level. I use this app a fair amount and I can see the results as well as email explanations of where the request got routed and when it’s likely to be addressed. They also give me links that explain policy and programs that the City has enacted. All that is really useful and it should be appreciated that the City cannot control the bad behavior and irresponsibility that cause many of these issues in the first place. They are reaching out to the general public for an extra set of eyes to help keep up. I think that’s great, I’m proud of our city for doing it and I hope they keep Findit Fixit going.
title: Great Community App

review: I’ve used this app multiple times and have received follow up several times, but the issues never fixed. The obvious one, illegal camping, the city won’t do anything about it, so they finally removed that as an option to report. Reported a giant dead rat on the sidewalk, they told me to bag it up and throw it in the trash. Nope. I’ve reported graffiti on a park mural that they never removed. Instead, a tent got set up there and eventually exploded with four propane tanks inside. This city is crumbling. As I redownloaded the app today to report a violent camper at a playground with parents removing their children who are just trying to enjoy their childhood, I remembered they removed the ability to report that, so I just gave up. Sad times. The concept of the app is great, but without a functioning city government, it’s pointless.
title: Good idea, but ineffective

review:
Confirmed with a parking supervisor. The expected turnaround time for parking enforcement request is 7 to 10 business days. Every few days they check for parking enforcement requests. Those are then reviewed by a parking supervisor. From there it’s scheduled with a parking enforcement officer. 7 to 10 business days is almost two weeks.

This app is useless unless the illegally parked vehicle is still there after 2 weeks.

I was told by the parking supervisor, for real-time enforcement requests, call the Seattle Police non-emergency number. Those requests are directly dispatched to parking enforcement officer in the field.
title: Useless for parking enforcement requests

review:
Looks like most all the low ratings are rants from people venting about the current state of the city. This app works very well, simple, intuitive, a very good way to efficiently communicate various common issues spotted in the streets of Seattle. I’ve fou t the crew on the other side of the app to be very responsive, either fixing dangerous potholes within a couple days, for instance, or quickly getting back to me to explain what was going on. 
No, it’s not a panacea, all pet peeves won’t always get fixed - through no fault of this app. 
Thanks for opening this handy communication channel between citizens and civil servants.
title: Convenient & fast communication with the city

review: I’ve reported dozens of instances of city roots raising sidewalks, creating tripping hazards. The city has fixed the majority of them within 24-48 hours of reporting them in some cases. The app isn’t perfect and some tickets for things like abandoned vehicles in an alley or overgrown brush blocking sidewalks don’t get addressed because it is up the homeowner or vehicle owner to deal with their crappy eyesores and lack of care. But at least it seems that whatever is in the power of the city to easily deal with gets logged and fixed fairly promptly. Even a pothole in an intersection was tagged for repair within a couple business days of my reporting it.
title: Sidewalk tripping hazards fixed within 24-48 hours sometimes

review: Our family with two kids live in a great seattle neighborhood with neat parks and lots of things to do. We LOVE using this app so we can productively file our concerns about illegal encampments, litter piling up, illegal parking, and illegal dumping to the city. We find it very effective in addressing the issues we see and they do get addressed. When they don’t, we submit another ticket. It works. Stop gossiping on next door and do something that can be truly tracked. You can help our neighborhoods be safe for families.
title: Stop Gossiping: Start Reporting the Issues

review: App won’t let me report a broken street lamp unless I go there and find a 7 digit number on some lamp pole. I gave address , photo, description, issue, and that wasn’t enough? If you know the address and have a photo of the issue why is that not enough?! App is trash, no wonder everything is broken! Make it so difficult to report a problem and then nobody will report, so nothing to fix right??!!

review:
I've only used it to report graffiti. I was surprised how easy it was, much better and faster than calling it in.  You can take a picture, use GPS, write descriptions, and give your contact info if you want updates. They came out in a few days and painted over the graffiti. 

Since you are sending so much info it's best to be on LTE or wifi when you submit, otherwise it takes a while.  There needs to be more apps like this.

review:
I was very skeptical about the City's ability to respond quickly to the volume of requests that would stem from an interface like this, but I was pleasantly surprised! I submitted a request for a tree to be trimmed that was dangerously blocking sight lines for drivers and pedestrians, and the work was done by close of business the same day.

I also like the clean and simple design of the app. If the city can keep up the good work, this will be a great tool.
title: Clean interface, fast resolution of issue!

review:
I have used this app to report my street for icy conditions. Signs were put up within hours. 
I have reported many pothole and steer issues and all have been fixed within days. So e potholes were even filled to cover the few days before the road was completely re paved.

title: Most of my tickets are ignored

review:
I have used this app for years to report everything from graffiti to stolen cars to hazardous materials from homeless encampments. The city does a great job fixing this stuff up quickly. 

My main critique is that this app needs to be promoted more via posters, billboards, Spotify, online ads, and more. An app is only as good as its user base, and most friends and family that I have talked with had never heard of this app before.

The other criticism that I have is that the team does need to always follow through with cleaning up the graffiti, fixing damaged roads, or removing debris to the full extent that it is actually there. It is frustrating to share a picture of some new ugly graffiti at a park, but only a small part of what is shown is actually cleaned up, and/or the rest of the park that has a lot more graffiti is left unchanged. Please try to do as much as you can while you are there - follow the broken window theory. Fix up as much as you can while you are there, don’t stick with the bare minimum.

I know that keeping the city in top-notch condition is a massive undertaking, I so try to share this app with everyone I know so that we can have a cleaner, healthier, and more beautiful city. Thank you to the City of Seattle for creating this app!
title: Great App with Excellent Potential

review: I have over a dozen SDOT concerns logged. Over last 60-100 days. No updates. And no work done. This a feel good app to make you think the city is listening to you. Don't waste your time. They could care less about citizen input that requires them to work on something other than what they want to do.

userName: Waste my time why don't you

review: Professionally, I clean up after the people who are living in tents and destroying their surrounding environments (our city) with endless garbage, urine, feces, heroine needles other drug paraphernalia and so much more. In the last 18 months, I’ve picked up over 1,400 33 gallon bags of trash from the homeless people who won’t pick up after themselves. Thanks to this app, every camper I’ve reported (& I only report the ones whose habits could potentially harm city residents) have been cleared away and usually within a week. THANK-YOU so much to our city workers. Our children deserve a livable & healthy city today and in the future.
title: I love this App!
